element_introduction_order.element: Four-button attack system (Light/Strong Punch and Kick)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial/early matches establish four-button configuration before special moves

element_introduction_order.element: Two-plane battle system with free plane movement
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn Plane Move (Light Punch + Light Kick simultaneous) in early matches
Introduced after basic four-button mastery; creates stage hazard risk
Forces strategic plane avoidance; prevents plane movement in affected stages
Optional mind-game element; no mechanical requirement

element_introduction_order.element: Desperation Move / Fury (Special Move at 25% health)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: High-risk/high-reward mechanic; requires near-death state to activate

element_introduction_order.element: Boss characters (Billy Kane, Axel Hawk, Laurence Blood, Wolfgang Krauser)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Final four fights; escalating difficulty with specialized movesets

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Within playable character roster (8 total matches)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Mirror match tests player's mastery of selected character

typical_relief_triggers: Bonus round after every fourth match (point accumulation)
Successful plane switch escape from hazard
typical_tension_triggers: Opponent entering stage hazard zone (bull stampede, electrified wires)
Player health dropping below 25% (Desperation Move availability)
Plane Move Attack attempts on opponent's plane
Boss character introduction (Billy Kane through Krauser progression)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Tournament bracket structure with escalating opponent difficulty; tension builds through character progression (8 playable → 4 bosses) with strategic pauses via bonus rounds; plane-switching mechanics create micro-tension cycles within each match

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus rounds appear after every fourth match (optional scoring opportunities)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Playable character roster (8 fighters) introduces core mechanics; early matches teach four-button system and basic plane movement
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Bonus rounds provide scoring variety; stage hazards introduce tactical depth (plane avoidance); Evasion Attack technique mastery required
difficulty_curve.late_game: Four non-playable boss characters with specialized movesets (Billy Kane's bojutsu, Axel Hawk's heavyweight boxing, Laurence Blood's bullfighting style, Wolfgang Krauser's nobility-based technique); Desperation Move mechanic becomes critical for survival
notable_spikes: Transition from playable to boss roster (difficulty leap from 8th playable match to Billy Kane)
Wolfgang Krauser final boss (searches for Geese killer; ultimate difficulty)
Stage hazard encounters (electrified wires or bull stampedes prevent plane switching, forcing aggressive play)

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player mode follows tournament structure: 8 playable characters + player clone = 9 matches, then 4 boss fights. Mysterious nobleman sponsor gradually defeats FF1 veterans while searching for Geese's killer.
After every fourth match, player enters bonus round for points. Provides pacing relief and scoring variety.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Bonus rounds at: match 4, match 8, match 12

notable_level_patterns.description: Certain stages contain background plane hazards (electrified wires, bull stampedes) that prevent plane switching. Forces player to either stay on safe plane or accept hazard damage via Power Attack knockback.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Australia stage (bull stampede confirmed in gameplay screenshot)

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Fatal Fury 2 SNES)
wikipedia (Fatal Fury 2 article - gameplay, plot, fighters, mechanics)
